pattern: Compressing the string into runs of equal characters lets each adjacent pair of runs contribute the smaller of their two lengths, summing to the count of valid grouped substrings.
hints: Any qualifying substring is one block of identical characters followed by an equally measured block of the other character.
Between two consecutive runs you can form min(previous run length, current run length) such balanced substrings.
Track the length of the current run and the previous run; each time the character changes, add min(prev, cur) to the answer.

edge_inputs.description: all same character, no valid substring
long leading run dwarfs short trailing run
single repeated character of length two

stress.description: random binary string with biased runs to vary block lengths
